decision: Josh's explicit decision, 2026-07-11: the app's KJV column corpus-wide switches from the 1611 original-spelling edition to the 1769 modern-spelling (Blayney-standard) edition, sourced from KJVA (scrollmapper/bible_databases, Sword Project provenance). See documentation/kjva-source-witness-intake.json and documentation/BIBLICAL_REMEDIATION_METHODOLOGY.md §1/§2 for full provenance and rationale.
whatThisSupersedes: Any registry entry recording a KJV trust claim (e.g. 'trusted_exact_source_collated') against the 1611 source at data/bible/translations/kjv-1611/source/aruljohn-bible-kjv-1611/ -- including but not necessarily limited to: greek-daniel-text-trust-status.json's additionKJV claim, greek-daniel-bound-addition-address-mapping.json's KJV mappings, greek-daniel-kjv-nabre-addition-exact-collation-result.json, greek-esther-bound-source-exact-collation-result.json's KJV scopes, and judith-bound-source-exact-collation-result.json's KJV scope (confirmed stale directly: 339-row 1611 collation, now superseded by a 1769 KJVA re-collation showing 36 addresses needing their own boundary-mapping work, analogous to the Daniel Prayer of Azariah case below).

Likely also stale, not yet individually confirmed: tobit-bound-source-exact-collation-result.json and wisdom-bound-source-exact-collation-result.json's KJV scopes, and any equivalent registry for Sirach, 1/2 Esdras, 1/2 Maccabees, and Prayer of Manasseh if such files exist -- not all checked yet as of this note.
whatRemainsValid: Every non-KJV trust claim in these registry files (NRSV, DRB, NABRE scopes, source-address policies for those translations, blocked/allowed-action lists for NRSV acquisition) is untouched by this switch and remains exactly as recorded.

workCompletedUnderNewSource.danielGK.json: All 14 chapters (ordinary Daniel 1-12, Prayer of Azariah insertion at 3:24-91, Susanna at ch.13, Bel and the Dragon at ch.14) re-collated against 1769 KJVA, verified at zero mismatches. One real find: active Daniel 3:52 merges KJVA Prayer of Azariah verses 29+30 into a single verse -- a naive constant-offset address mapping would have silently produced wrong content past that point despite reporting zero missing addresses; caught by content-level verification, not key-existence alone.
workCompletedUnderNewSource.estherGK.json: Ordinary Esther (numbered chapters) plus Additions A-F all re-collated against 1769 KJVA (Additions to Esther, chapters 10-16), verified at zero mismatches. This is new KJV coverage for the Additions specifically -- confirmed via greek-esther-bound-source-exact-collation-result.json that KJV text for the named additions was previously entirely absent, not previously trusted-then-overwritten.
workCompletedUnderNewSource.newStandaloneFiles: susanna.json (64 verses), belandthedragon.json (42 verses), prayerofazariah.json (67 verses, using the app's existing NRSV/DRB/NABRE verse-merge convention at the 29+30 boundary), additionstoesther.json (6 lettered chapters, 105 verses) -- created per Josh's decision to make these searchable as standalone units for Anglican/Protestant use, mirroring KJVA's own book divisions, while danielGK.json and estherGK.json remain as the merged Catholic/Orthodox-canon forms.

correctionToEarlierMischaracterization: Susanna's active Daniel 13:65 and Baruch 3:38 are NOT stray/empty-verse bugs, despite being described that way in an earlier same-day patch commit message. They are deliberately governed, policy-documented address rows -- Daniel 13:65 holds real DRB-only content (an Astyages/Cyrus transition row per greek-daniel-drb-addition-source-address-policy.json's explicit reasoning) that NRSV/KJV/NABRE are correctly absent from pending an approved NRSV source witness. Baruch 3:38 has not been independently re-checked against an equivalent policy file as of this note, but given the exact parallel pattern, it should NOT be assumed to be a simple empty-verse defect without checking for a similar governing policy first.
nextRequiredWork: Systematically check whether Judith, Tobit, Sirach, 1/2 Esdras, 1/2 Maccabees, Wisdom, and Prayer of Manasseh each have an existing bound-source-exact-collation-result.json (or equivalent) describing 1611-based KJV address mappings, before re-deriving 1769 KJVA boundary mappings from scratch for each.

For each book with residual 'missing' addresses after the mechanical 1769 KJVA replacement, apply the same discipline used for Daniel's Prayer of Azariah: locate the exact content-level boundary via direct comparison, not by assuming a constant verse-count offset.
Re-check Baruch 3:38 against baruch-source-address-policy.json or equivalent, if one exists, before treating it as a defect.
Update (not silently leave) the specific stale registry files listed above once each book's re-collation against 1769 KJVA is complete, following this note's pattern.
